reasoning: The ECB has been conducting Quantitative Tightening (QT), running off its APP and PEPP portfolios, which puts its balance sheet on a reliable structural downturn (approximately -10 billion EUR/week). For the 1-week horizon (2025-11-02), visibility is very high and the QT trend should prevail over normal weekly liquidity (MRO) noise, resulting in a low probability of increase (25%). Over the 1-month horizon (2025-11-25), while accumulated QT runoff is larger, forecasting uncertainty grows due to potential unpredicted changes in bank reserve demands or lumpy operational settlements, pulling the probability closer to 0.5 (36%). Over the 3-month horizon (2026-01-24), high epistemic uncertainty regarding winter liquidity needs, unforeseen macroeconomic events, and potential policy pivots dominates, drawing the probability even closer to 0.5 as per the principle of increasing uncertainty over distant horizons (45%).
